results.title: Something creative
results.creator_credit: Gillian Leahy and Crafts Council of Australia
results.brief_description: This film looks at the question of creativity in everyday life and examines the role of the professional artist in stimulating and encouraging participation in the arts by the community at large. Focuses on Orange and Blacktown in New South Wales. Cinematography by Erika Addis.

results.title: Surviving the Outback Rivers
results.brief_description: The vast Australian Outback has been described as a land of heroes and dead men. Join Scott Lambert and seven Australian army commandoes who are all recipients of the green beret on a three week rafting expedition, where enough provisions for one week have been taken, with the idea of “living off the land” for most of the time. Setting out from Darwin, the journey will take these adventurers down the Katherine River through the Katherine Gorge, then back through the Clarevale Crossing and the Daly River Crossing, back to Darwin. A fascinating adventure amongst some of the most beautiful wilderness in Australia.

results.title: Would you like gravy with that?
results.brief_description:
A short created by a student for the 2004 ACMI Diegesis secondary school media festival.
This work depicts the views of students, teachers and the filmmakers on the state of the De La Salle College canteen. The owner of the canteen is interviewed, as well as students and teachers who use the canteen.

results.title: Silk screen printing
results.creator_credit: Josef Bohmer and Harry A Kapit
results.brief_description: An introduction to silk screen printing, using ordinary paints and paper. Although the film does not deal with the use of dyes and the process of printing on fabrics, it covers adequately the more elementary uses of the process, showing the cutting of the stencil (a lacquer stencil), the removal of the top layer, fitting the stencil to the screen, and drying the finished prints - in this case Christmas and greeting cards.

results.title: The Dance of the angels: ceramic sculptures of John Perceval
results.creator_credit: Tim Burstall, Patrick Ryan and Eltham Films
results.brief_description: Camera movement and film editing are combined to animate the ceramic figurines of John Perceval. He created his angels “more or less as symbols of the world’s survival.” An intangible element common to all Perceval’s angels is their spirit of humanity. These ceramic sculptures are disting- uished by their beautiful modelling, and their lustrous red copper glaze with its uncanny depth and beauty. Perceval does not devote himself exclusively to ceramic sculpture; he gives equal time to painting and has won the McCaughey and Vizard-Whollohan Prizes, and shared the Wynne Prize in 1961. Photography by Gerard Vanderberg; music by Dorian Le Gallienne.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Tim Burstall, Patrick Ryan and Eltham Films

results.title: Fair treatment
results.creator_credit: Tim Johnson, Chris McGill and Swinburne Institute of Technology. Film and Television School
results.brief_description: This program examines some of the most important issues facing people living with HIV and contains interviews with people who are HIV positive. It focuses on the availability of drugs for HIV patients, and the lack of action and apathy shown by Australian governments. It also explores the outlook on life of the interviewees, how the stigma of AIDS, the death of friends and loved ones has affected them and finally examines their attitudes to death and dying.

results.title: Ross Gibson interview: tape #1
results.brief_description:
Interview Date: 21st March, 2002.
Ross Gibson takes the interviewers through the ACMI site discussing issues regarding the conception of the building, ideas and philosophies behind the interaction between the public and the space, working processes, anticipated audience experiences and, a general background of the history of the planning and construction of the Centre.

results.title: True faith
results.creator_credit: Lawrence Johnston and Swinburne Institute of Technology. Film and Television School
results.brief_description: True Faith is a dramatic examination of fidelity and relationships in the era of AIDS. At a nightclub, Alan bumps into Stewart, a past lover who has just recently returned to Melbourne. Stewart has been tested as HIV-Positive and his lover has recently died as a result of AIDS. Allan decides to get tested for AIDS. This is a delicately handled drama about a young man coming to terms with an HIV-Positive result, and how to break the news to his partner. Cast includes Dale Knight, Eric Scherer, Colin Batrouney.
